title: Smoke-Cold Indian Galangal Dal
description: A comforting, creamy, and aromatic Indian dal made with galangal, perfect for a chilly evening. This flexitarian-friendly dish combines the flavors of smoke-cold spices with the earthy taste of galangal.
instructions: Rinse the lentils and soak them in water for at least 4 hours.
Peel and chop the galangal into small pieces and sauté them in oil until slightly softened.
Add the onion and sauté until translucent.
Add the ginger and sauté for another minute.
Add the cumin seeds, coriander seeds, garam masala, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and sauté for 1 minute.
Drain the water from the lentils and add them to the pot with 4 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Let the dal simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the lentils are tender.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the dal hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Refrigerate the dal for at least 4 hours or overnight to allow the flavors to meld and the texture to become creamy.
Serve the smoke-cold dal chilled, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
